user_prompt: I want a project wide removal of SD and we are changing to using SdFat instead. VFS paths are not important. What is important is that the files being created have actual creation time stamp file attributes so we can sort them later.
agent_reply: Implemented the project-wide SD to SdFat move.
- Updated GUI image SD fallback to use SdFat instead of Arduino `SD`: [FlyGui.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp)

Timestamp handling is now centralized through `FsDateTime::setCallback(...)`, using `ClockAgent` to populate FAT creation/sync timestamps via `FS_DATE` / `FS_TIME`. I also removed the VFS path API and POSIX `truncate()` usage; files are now truncated through `FsFile::truncate()` while still open.
I verified there are no remaining `#include <SD.h>` or `SD.` references. I couldn’t run a PlatformIO build because `pio` is not installed/on PATH here, and `python -m platformio` is unavailable.

References used for SdFat timestamp/preallocation behavior: [SdFat GitHub](https://github.com/greiman/SdFat), [SdFat timestamp callback notes](https://github-wiki-see.page/m/renesas/Arduino/wiki/How-to-set-timestamps-on-files-handled-by-SdFat).

user_prompt: Please implement the code as described by the comment on lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.h:1
I need one function that returns the 6 most recently created files on the microSD card's root directory
Remember that the recorder class already adds the file attributes, so it will be available, ignore files that do not have the attribute.
The return should be a list of strings, each string can be an array that is probably just a bit longer than the maximum possible file name according to our file name specs
Replace the comment with your implementation.
Optimize for speed. Don't over-explore or over-check your work other than what is absolutely necessary to implement the comment.
Once you are done, remove the comment.
agent_reply: Implemented `MostRecentFiles::get()` in [MostRecentFiles.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.cpp) and replaced the TODO comment in [MostRecentFiles.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/MostRecentFiles.h).

It now returns a fixed `FileList` containing up to 6 root-directory file names, sorted newest-first by creation date/time, filtering to regular files with `FS_ATTRIB_ARCHIVE`. It scans once and keeps only the top six candidates.

Verification: I tried `pio run`. It failed before reaching this new file because of an existing `SdCard` namespace collision with SdFat’s `SdCard` typedef in `src/mic_sd_recorder_demo.cpp` / `lib/SdCard/SdCard.h`.

user_prompt: I don't remember ever creating an internal library called SdCard
agent_reply: It is in the repo history, not something I invented today.
`git ls-files lib/SdCard` shows both files are tracked:
And `git log -- lib/SdCard` shows they came from commit:

So my read is: it was introduced in that work-in-progress commit as a small wrapper around the microSD `SdFs` instance. You may not remember creating it because it looks like one of those “obvious helper module” names that can slip in during a broader pass. The original name `SdCard` just happened to collide badly with SdFat’s own global `SdCard` typedef.
